turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Hi, doctor. I'm here for my blood pressure follow-up. I've also been getting some headaches lately.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Good morning. I’m glad you came in. Let’s talk through it carefully. How have you been feeling overall, and when did the headaches start?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Overall I’m okay. The headaches started about two weeks ago. They come and go, not every day.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Where do you feel the headache, and what does it feel like? Any nausea, blurred vision, weakness, or trouble speaking?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: It's mostly around my forehead, like a pressure. No vomiting. No weakness or speech problems. My vision is fine.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That is helpful. Does anything seem to bring it on, like stress, poor sleep, long screen time, or skipping meals? And what helps it settle?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Work has been busy, so I do get stressed. I sit at a computer a lot. Sometimes paracetamol helps, and rest helps too.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Understood. Have you checked your blood pressure at home or elsewhere recently? And how have you been taking the amlodipine?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I take the amlodipine every day, yes. I’ve missed maybe one dose in the last month. I checked it at a pharmacy last week and it was a bit high, around 148 over 92.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thank you. Any chest pain, shortness of breath, palpitations, swelling in the legs, or dizziness?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No chest pain or shortness of breath. I do not feel dizzy. Maybe I’ve been a bit tired, but that’s all.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Alright. Let me ask a bit about your routine. How is your sleep, caffeine intake, and alcohol on weekends?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Sleep is not great some nights. I have two cups of coffee in the morning. On weekends I might have a few drinks, but not every weekend.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thanks. And just to complete the picture, do you smoke, and do you have any chance of being pregnant?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, I don’t smoke. And no, I’m not pregnant.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Good. Your headaches sound more like tension-type headaches than something dangerous, and your blood pressure still seems a little above target. I’d like to check your blood pressure here today, review your readings, and consider whether we should adjust your treatment. We should also look at weight, salt intake, exercise, and sleep. If the pressure is still raised, we may increase the amlodipine or add another medicine, depending on the reading and your exam. Please come back sooner if you get a sudden severe headache, chest pain, weakness, blurred vision, or if the blood pressure is very high. Otherwise, we’ll plan follow-up after today’s measurements.
